message.content:
PLAYER: "Scrodinger" (multiple accounts).
RATINGS: chess.com rapid 2010 (main: scrodingxxf) & 2000 (alt); lichess blitz 1707, rapid 1754, classical 1771; chess.com blitz best 1612, currently lower; bullet ~1150.
TACTICS/PUZZLES: chess.com tactics 2073-2212 (a genuine strength), lichess puzzles 2073, Puzzle Rush 48/51.
ESTIMATED FIDE OTB: ~1600-1700. Has NOT played FIDE-rated OTB yet.
REPERTOIRE — White: 1.e4 player. vs 1...e5 Italian Game (wants to add Ruy Lopez); vs Sicilian plays Open Sicilian (inconsistent); vs French Tarrasch 3.Nd2; vs Caro-Kann Advance 3.e5.
REPERTOIRE — Black: vs 1.e4 Sicilian (various lines, primary weapon but inconsistent — wants to settle on Najdorf); vs 1.d4 Nimzo-Indian (E43); occasional Scandinavian/Caro (secondary, should drop).
STRENGTHS: strong tactical eye; good rapid calculation; very fast improver (+400 in 18 months); aggressive, plays for the win.
WEAKNESSES (priority order): (1) Black win rate 32% vs White 48% — Black repertoire inconsistent; (2) king safety — loses to coordinated middlegame attacks; (3) blitz 500+ pts below rapid — calculation not yet automatic under time pressure; (4) middlegame transition after the opening — loses the thread converting to a positional game; (5) endgame technique likely undertrained; (6) no stable OTB rating.
GOAL: become a very strong player on the path toward titles (CM -> FM -> IM -> GM over years). Wants a personalized course/resource curriculum.


YOU ARE A CHESS-RESOURCE RESEARCHER. Category: "Interactive Platforms & Training Tools".

Find the best interactive PLATFORMS and TOOLS for training and analysis: Chessable (spaced-repetition — explain MoveTrainer), Aimchess (weakness analytics), Chessmood, iChess, Chess.com Insights/lessons, lichess Studies + analysis + opening explorer, ChessBase (PC database software) + Mega Database, DecodeChess (AI explanations), and any modern AI-coaching tools. Explain what each is best at and how Scrodinger should use it. Include game-analysis workflow tools.

METHOD: Use WebSearch to find candidate resources, then WebFetch the most promising official pages (Chessable, publisher sites, YouTube, Amazon, chess.com, lichess) to confirm titles, authors, prices, and URLs. Do MULTIPLE searches with varied queries. Return 6-12 REAL, currently-available resources. Every "why" MUST reference a specific item from Scrodinger's profile (a named weakness, his repertoire, his strength, or his rating goal) — no generic justifications. Be concrete with prices and canonical URLs. Mark each Essential/Recommended/Optional.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Aimchess, its weakness analytics features, and pricing:

## Overview

Aimchess is an AI-powered chess analytics and training platform that creates personalized puzzles and lessons based on analysis of your own games from Chess.com, Lichess, or Chess24.

## Weakness Analytics Features

Aimchess imports your games from Chess.com or Lichess, analyzes them statistically across hundreds of games, and identifies patterns in your mistakes — not just individual blunders but recurring patterns. The platform analyzes performance across six core aspects such as tactics, endgame, advantage capitalization, resourcefulness, time management, and opening performance.

The platform analyzes your games, identifies patterns in your errors, and creates daily puzzles that target your specific weaknesses. The plan adjusts dynamically based on your performance. If you consistently solve certain puzzle types correctly, the system reduces their frequency. If you continue making errors in specific patterns, the algorithm increases exposure to those positions. This creates a feedback loop that targets your actual weak points rather than theoretical ones.

## Pricing

Pricing for Aimchess varies across sources:

- Aimchess analyzes your Lichess or Chess.com statistics with the help of AI analytics. Available for $5-$7 a month
- The meaningful features — the personalized weakness analysis, the daily drills, the full opening performance breakdown — are locked behind a subscription that runs around $12-15 per month (as of early 2026; pricing changes).
- Free · from $7.99/mo

Aimchess' Free Tier allows you to do any 15 lessons per day in the Training Room or via the daily Personalized Workouts. You also get limited access to Scouting Reports and Statistics. Aimchess Premium allows you access to unlimited exercises in the Training Room and full access to all of our Statistical Analysis tools.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about DecodeChess:

## What is DecodeChess?

DecodeChess introduces Explainable AI (XAI) to the world of chess analysis, by providing in-depth explanations to a chess engine's moves. Until today, chess engines could tell you what the next best chess move is, but they couldn't explain why that move was chosen.

## How It Works

DecodeChess combines the merits of a chess master and one of the strongest chess engines available (Stockfish NNUE). The platform offers two main analysis features:

- **Decode Game**: Analyze an entire game and receive explanations for the recommended moves of a chess engine (Stockfish). This only takes about 60 seconds!

- **Decode Deep**: Analyze a chess position in-depth to receive a 360° analysis of threats, attacking plans, concepts, piece functionality, good moves, and more.

## Pricing

DecodeChess pricing starts at $8.25/monthly and $84 annually. You can also choose a one-time payment where you can get access to features and functionalities via credits. Additionally, in decode chess, you will get 2 free games to analyze every day.

## Who It's For

DecodeChess is a next-generation chess analysis tool that explains the reasoning and concepts behind the suggested moves of a chess engine (Stockfish), promoting chess understanding for players with an ELO rating of up to 2000.

Based on the search results, here's information about ChessMood's opening courses, GM membership, and pricing for 2025:

## ChessMood Membership Plans & Pricing (2025)

ChessMood offers two membership plans: the Essential plan at $99/month or $79/month annually, and the Pro plan at $129/month or $99/month annually (which includes extras like a 1-on-1 GM call).

## Opening Courses

Both memberships grant access to an extensive range of chess courses, most of which are recorded by GM Avetik Grigoryan and GM Gabuzyan Hovhannes.

The platform offers Simplified ChessMood Openings for the 800-2000 level, allowing players to learn a fun and fundamental opening repertoire without memorizing long variations. With the simplified ChessMood openings—WhiteMood and BlackMood courses—players can learn everything needed to dominate the opening phase below the 2,000 level while understanding the underlying strategies and plans for each position.

## Additional Features

ChessMood offers a no-questions-asked 30-Day Money-Back Guarantee, giving you an entire month to explore Grandmaster-crafted courses, practice with training exercises, and if you don't feel tangible improvement in your gameplay, you can request a full refund.

Based on the search results, here's an overview of Lichess's free features for Studies, the Analysis Board, and the Opening Explorer:

## Analysis Board

The Lichess analysis board allows you to analyze chess positions and variations on an interactive chess board.

## Opening Explorer

The Lichess Opening Explorer lets you search through over 12 million Lichess games (and counting) with more than 390 million unique positions, and it also features a specially curated database of 2 million master games from thousands of over-the-board tournaments since 1952—all completely free.

The Opening Explorer can be accessed by going directly to the Analysis Board or by going to the analysis view of any game and clicking on the "Book" icon below the moves list. As the position on the board changes, the Opening Explorer will update to show popular moves, along with additional information such as average player rating, the number of times the position has been reached, and the game result distributions.

## Studies

Lichess studies are a great feature where you can collect games, save your analysis, and share with others if you played the game on Lichess or have the PGN from another source.

## Free Access

You have access to all these features for free, without needing to purchase Chessbase and a database of games. Lichess is free forever according to the founder and it is open source.

Based on the search results, here's information about Chess.com's Diamond membership, features, and pricing as of 2025:

## Diamond Membership Features

Chess.com's Diamond membership includes unlimited Puzzles, Game Reviews, and Insights. Diamond members receive all the benefits of lower tiers and additionally get unlimited insights and Coach Explanations, which are AI-generated inputs that help you learn more from each game you play.

## Pricing

The Diamond membership in the US costs $120 USD per year. However, memberships are cheapest in Africa, South Asia, and Southeast Asia, and most expensive across Western Europe and Scandinavia. The pattern roughly mirrors local purchasing power.

## Friends & Family Plan

Chess.com released a Family & Friends plan in May 2025. This plan allows 6 people in total (you and 5 others) to join the plan and receive Diamond-level membership perks. You and your loved ones can enjoy all Premium benefits with a discount of up to 70% off.

## Membership Structure Changes

Chess.com removed Platinum and added a new plan called Friends and Family. They removed Platinum which had an extra feature of unlimited game review.

Links: [{"title":"PRO Membership - Chessable","url":"https://www.chessable.com/pro/"},{"title":"Chessable PRO Price Changes - Chessable Blog","url":"https://www.chessable.com/blog/chessable-pro-price-changes/"},{"title":"Improving Your Chess: Cost and Quality | US Chess.org","url":"https://new.uschess.org/news/improving-chess-cost-quality"},{"title":"Changes to Chessable and Chessable PRO - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/blog/Chessable/new-year-big-changes-to-chessable-pro"},{"title":"Pro membership price","url":"https://www.chessable.com/discussion/thread/8880/pro-membership-price/"},{"title":"Pro Subscription - Chessable","url":"https://www.chessable.com/discussion/thread/305549/pro-subscription/"},{"title":"Pro Membership","url":"https://www.chessable.com/discussion/thread/295210/pro-membership/"},{"title":"Chessable","url":"https://www.chessable.com/discussion/thread/177129/pricing-concerns/"},{"title":"Chessable Discounts Return to 2024 System; PRO Discount Now 30% – Chessable Blog","url":"https://www.chessable.com/blog/chessable-discounts-return-to-2024-system-pro-discount-now-30/"},{"title":"Pro Membership - should I get it? - Chessable","url":"https://www.chessable.com/discussion/thread/174289/pro-membership-should-i-get-it/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Chessable PRO membership pricing and what's included:

## Pricing (2025)

The monthly membership is $11.99, and the yearly package is $74.99.

## What's Included

PRO members get an automatic 20% discount on any Chessable course. However, this has been updated more recently—PRO members now have a minimum 30% discount on all courses at all times (as of September 2025).

Additional benefits mentioned in the search results include perks like course discounts and access to exclusive features, though the full detailed list of all PRO benefits wasn't fully displayed in the search results. For a complete breakdown of all PRO membership features, you may want to visit the official Chessable PRO membership page at chessable.com/pro/.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# ChessBase 18 Mega Package

**Price:** €349.90

**Contents:** The package includes four main components:

1. ChessBase 18 Program
2. "1-year Premium membership"
3. Mega Database 2025
4. "Annual subscription to ChessBase Magazine (6 issues, digital)"

Additionally, it features an update service through December 31, 2025, which provides approximately 5,000 games weekly.

content.text: I have enough confirmed data across all the major platforms. Let me compile the final structured output.

resources.name: Chessable PRO (with MoveTrainer)
resources.cost: PRO $11.99/mo or $74.99/yr (free tier exists; many courses sold separately)
resources.why: MoveTrainer's spaced-repetition is the single best fix for Scrodinger's #1 weakness: an inconsistent Black repertoire (32% win rate). He can drill a Najdorf course and his Nimzo-Indian (E43) until the lines are automatic recall, exactly the consistency he lacks. PRO's 30% course discount pays for itself once he buys the Najdorf + Ruy Lopez (White upgrade he wants) courses.

resources.name: Aimchess
resources.why: Aimchess imports his chess.com (scrodingxxf) and lichess games and statistically pinpoints recurring patterns across six areas including time management. That directly targets his blitz gap (500+ pts below rapid) by quantifying whether errors are calculation or clock-driven, and its weakness-targeted daily workouts attack his king-safety and middlegame-transition leaks instead of random puzzles.

resources.name: Chess.com Diamond (Game Review, Insights, Lessons)
resources.why: He already plays his main games on chess.com (rapid 2010), so unlimited Game Review + Insights + AI Coach Explanations turn his own losses into a feedback loop — specifically flagging the king-safety collapses and post-opening drift he's named as weaknesses. Insights also exposes his Black-vs-White result split numerically so he can track the 32% Black win-rate as he fixes it.

resources.name: Lichess Studies + Analysis Board + Opening Explorer
resources.why: Free Opening Explorer over 2M master games + the Studies tool let him build and stress-test a settled Najdorf repertoire (his stated goal) and his Open Sicilian as White, which he plays inconsistently. He can save annotated Studies of his own Sicilian games to nail down the middlegame plans he loses the thread on after the opening.

resources.name: DecodeChess
resources.why: DecodeChess explains WHY Stockfish's move is best (threats, plans, piece functionality) rather than just an eval number. That addresses his #4 weakness — losing the thread in the middlegame transition — by translating engine lines into the positional concepts a tactically-strong-but-positionally-raw 2000 player like him needs to internalize on the road past 2000.

resources.name: ChessMood (Essential/Pro membership)
resources.cost: Essential $99/mo or $79/mo annual; Pro $129/mo or $99/mo annual; 30-day money-back
resources.why: GM-recorded repertoire courses give him a coherent, plan-based 1.e4 system (he's a 1.e4 player wanting to add the Ruy Lopez and stabilize the Open Sicilian) plus aggressive Black weapons that match his win-seeking style. The structured curriculum and accountability suit his goal of climbing toward CM/FM titles rather than the piecemeal study causing his repertoire inconsistency.

resources.name: ChessBase 18 Mega Package (incl. Mega Database 2025)
resources.why: As he pushes toward FIDE OTB and titles, the 11M-game Mega Database is the professional standard for deep opening preparation — letting him build serious files on his Najdorf and Nimzo-Indian and scout future OTB opponents, something free explorers can't fully replace. Best deferred until he commits to rated OTB given the price.

resources.name: lichess Puzzle Streak / Puzzle Storm + Custom Puzzle Themes
resources.why: His tactics are a genuine strength (lichess puzzles 2073, Puzzle Rush 48/51) but his blitz is 500+ pts below rapid because calculation isn't automatic under time pressure. Timed Puzzle Storm specifically trains fast pattern recognition under the clock — converting his proven tactical accuracy into the speed his blitz lacks.

resources.name: Chessable Tactics & Endgame courses (via MoveTrainer)
resources.why: His endgame technique is likely undertrained (self-identified #5 weakness) and spaced-repetition is ideal for memorizing exact endgame technique. A course like '100 Endgames You Must Know' on MoveTrainer drills theoretical endings to permanent recall — the kind of conversion technique that separates a 2000 player from a titled one.

content.text:
Research complete. I compiled 9 verified, currently-available interactive platforms and training tools for Scrodinger, each tied to a specific item from his profile.

Key findings (all prices confirmed against official pages June 2026):

**Essential (4):**
- **Chessable PRO + MoveTrainer** ($11.99/mo, $74.99/yr) — spaced repetition to fix his #1 weakness, the inconsistent Black repertoire (32% win rate); drill Najdorf + Nimzo to automatic recall.
- **Aimchess** (free tier / ~$7.99-12 Premium) — imports his chess.com + lichess games, statistically isolates whether his blitz collapse is calculation vs. clock.
- **Chess.com Diamond** (~$120/yr) — he already plays there (rapid 2010); unlimited Game Review + Insights expose his king-safety losses and the Black/White result split numerically.
- **Lichess Studies + Opening Explorer** (free) — build and stress-test a settled Najdorf and his Open Sicilian.

**Recommended (4):** DecodeChess (explainable-AI for his middlegame-transition weakness), ChessMood (GM repertoire courses for his 1.e4 + Ruy Lopez goal), Lichess Puzzle Storm (turn his strong tactics into blitz speed), Chessable endgame/tactics courses (his undertrained endgame).

**Optional (1):** ChessBase 18 Mega Package (€349.90) — professional-grade prep tool to defer until he commits to rated OTB.

A few confirmed price points worth flagging: Chessable PRO now carries a 30%+ standing course discount (as of Sept 2025); DecodeChess is explicitly tuned for players up to ~2000 Elo, which fits him precisely; ChessBase Mega Database 2025 holds 11M+ games.

The full structured data has been returned via the StructuredOutput tool.
